sections.paragraph: & הֲוָא Ch. to be, i.q. Heb. הָיָה. Fut. יֶהֱוֵה and יֶהֱוֵא. To this future there is sometimes prefixed the particle לְ, which then means that, in order that, and the preformative of the future is commonly omitted, as לֶהֱֹון that they may be, that they might be, Dan 2:43 6:2 , לֶהֶוְיָן Dan 5:17 compare Winer Ch. Gramm. § 44, 4. It is often joined with the participle of another verb, and thus forms a commonly used circumlocution for the aorist; חָזֵה הֲוֵית “thou wast seeing,” Dan 4:7, 10 7:2 , , etc.
